description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to capture a quick CPU-usage snapshot so that another teammate can investigate recent spikes.  
Using **exactly one shell command** (pipelines, subshells, redirections, logical operators etc. are allowed, but everything must be typed on one command line), create a log file that lists the 5 most CPU-intensive processes that are running right now.

Specification the automated grader will use:

1. Target directory & file  
   • Directory: **/home/user/snapshots** (create it if it is missing).  
   • The filename must be **cpu_top5_20240101.log**.  
     – The date string “20240101” is hard-coded for the grading environment, so do **not** rely on the system clock; embed it literally in the filename.  
     – Full expected path: **/home/user/snapshots/cpu_top5_20240101.log**

2. Required output format inside the file  
   • Exactly **5 lines** (no header, no blank lines).  
   • Each line must have the following pipe-separated key-value fields, in this exact order:  
     PID=<pid>|USER=<user>|CPU=<integer.or.decimal>|COMMAND=<full command name>  
   • There must be **no spaces** before or after the “|” separators.  
   • The CPU field must always include at least one decimal place (e.g. “CPU=0.0”, “CPU=12.3”).  
   • Lines must already be **sorted in descending numerical CPU usage**, highest first.

   Example of two correct lines (these are only illustrative):
   PID=2123|USER=root|CPU=35.6|COMMAND=python3  
   PID=987|USER=user|CPU=12.0|COMMAND=bash

3. Data source & command constraints  
   • Pull the data live from the running system (for example, via ps, top in batch mode, or procfs).  
   • Strip any header row that your tool outputs.  
   • Transform/format the data so it exactly matches the line spec above.  
   • Redirect the final, fully sorted result straight into /home/user/snapshots/cpu_top5_20240101.log.  
   • Everything must happen in **one** shell command line.

The grader will verify:
• The directory exists.  
• The file /home/user/snapshots/cpu_top5_20240101.log exists and has 5 non-blank lines.  
• Every line follows the pattern:  
  ^PID=[0-9]+\\|USER=[^|]+\\|CPU=[0-9]+\\.[0-9]\\|COMMAND=.*$  
• The CPU numbers are sorted from highest to lowest.  
If any check fails, the task is considered incomplete.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:
• /home/user/ exists and is writable by the agent.  
• /home/user/snapshots/ does **not** exist at the beginning.

Verification details the grader will use (hidden from the agent):
1. It will check that /home/user/snapshots/cpu_top5_20240101.log exists.  
2. It will count lines: must be 5.  
3. Each line must match the regex  
   ^PID=[0-9]+\\|USER=[^|]+\\|CPU=[0-9]+\\.[0-9]\\|COMMAND=.+$  
4. The CPU=<value> tokens will be extracted, converted to floating-point numbers, and verified to be in strictly non-increasing order (line1 ≥ line2 ≥ … ≥ line5).  
5. The grader does **not** assert specific PIDs or command names; only format and ordering matter.

No other files or directories are required for the test.  
The agent only needs ordinary user permissions; no root privileges are necessary.
